radiologist: Dr. Karen Bowen (Cardiology — Echo lab attending)
indication: Hospital day 2 — assess for cardiac involvement of SLE/myositis. CT noted trace pericardial effusion; rule out tamponade physiology; evaluate myocardial function.
technique: Standard 2D and Doppler transthoracic echocardiogram with M-mode, color flow, pulsed and continuous wave Doppler, and tissue Doppler imaging. Adequate acoustic windows obtained despite body habitus.
findings:
LEFT VENTRICLE:
- LV size: Normal. End-diastolic dimension 4.2 cm, end-systolic 2.8 cm.
- LV wall thickness: Normal (septum 0.8 cm, posterior wall 0.8 cm).
- LV systolic function: Normal. Ejection fraction 60% (biplane Simpson's method).
- LV regional wall motion: No regional wall motion abnormalities.
- LV diastolic function: Mild Grade I diastolic dysfunction (E/A ratio 0.7, deceleration time 230 ms, e' average 8 cm/s; E/e' 9).
- No left ventricular hypertrophy.
- No thrombus visualized.

RIGHT VENTRICLE:
- RV size and function: Normal. RV basal diameter 3.0 cm. TAPSE 18 mm.
- No RV regional wall motion abnormalities.

ATRIA:
- Left atrium: Normal size (LA volume index 22 mL/m²).
- Right atrium: Normal size.

VALVES:
- Aortic valve: Tri-leaflet, normal opening, no stenosis, no regurgitation.
- Mitral valve: Normal leaflets, no prolapse, no regurgitation. No mitral stenosis.
- Tricuspid valve: Normal. Trace tricuspid regurgitation (physiologic). Peak TR velocity 2.4 m/s; estimated RVSP 28 mmHg (normal).
- Pulmonic valve: Normal. No stenosis or regurgitation.
- No vegetations identified on any valve.

PERICARDIUM:
- TRACE PERICARDIAL EFFUSION (approximately 3-4 mm) circumferentially, predominantly posterior.
- No echocardiographic signs of tamponade (no diastolic right atrial collapse, no diastolic right ventricular collapse, IVC normal size and respiratory variation).
- No pericardial thickening.

GREAT VESSELS:
- Aortic root: Normal (2.8 cm at sinus of Valsalva).
- Inferior vena cava: 1.4 cm with >50% respiratory variation — estimated RA pressure 3 mmHg (normal).
- Pulmonary artery: Normal.

ADDITIONAL:
- No intracardiac mass or thrombus.
- No congenital abnormalities.
- Cachectic body habitus visible on echo.
impression:
1. NORMAL LEFT VENTRICULAR SYSTOLIC FUNCTION. LV EF 60% with no regional wall motion abnormalities. No evidence of myocarditis on this study (no regional dysfunction or wall motion abnormality). NOTE: focal myocardial involvement of myositis may not always be apparent on echo; consider cardiac MRI if clinical concern persists.

2. MILD GRADE I DIASTOLIC DYSFUNCTION — non-specific, may reflect mild interstitial changes or simply small heart size in cachectic patient.

3. TRACE PERICARDIAL EFFUSION without tamponade physiology. Consistent with SLE serositis or hypoalbuminemia-related transudative effusion. No intervention needed.

4. NO VALVULAR VEGETATIONS to suggest Libman-Sacks endocarditis or infective endocarditis. (Libman-Sacks lesions can be small and missed; transesophageal study would be more sensitive but not indicated currently.)

5. NO ELEVATED PULMONARY ARTERY PRESSURE.

6. NORMAL RV SIZE AND FUNCTION.

Overall: REASSURING cardiac evaluation. No evidence of significant acute cardiac involvement of SLE/myositis on this study. The trace pericardial effusion does not require drainage. Recommend repeat echocardiogram in 2-4 weeks if clinical change.
